learning_outcomes.description: Differentiate between developed and developing countries by analysing current conditions, such as labour markets, poverty and seasonality.
Explain the impact of globalization on poverty, inequality, and growth in the developing world.
Analyse the development policy-making process recognizing the relative roles of the market, state, and civil society.
Analyse how the unique environment faced by households in the developing world shapes their behaviour.
Explain how we measure development outcomes, with a focus on the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

assessments.task_description:
Journal Article Review (JAR) Guidelines and Instructions

Word Limit:  1480 - 1520 words (excluding references in the list, in-text references, and section headings).

Review ONE Journal Article of Your Choice. The articles that you choose will need to have prior approval from the course coordinator by 5 pm (BRISBANE TIME (GMT+10 HOURS) on 1 September 2026 at the latest. JAR constitutes a critically important building block in learning outcomes for this course and complements other components of assessment in ECON2560, especially the Country Report. You are required to complete this task individually, not in a group. 

Students may send in a draft Review for feedback prior to the due date.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission via turn-it-in only in the course Blackboard by the due date. No other form of submission will be ACCEPTABLE and will be treated as a NON-SUBMISSION. Brisbane time (GMT +10 hours) must be followed.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Country Paper: Guidelines and Instructions

Students are required to write a paper of approximately 3,000 words in length (a minimum of 2,975 words and a maximum of 3,025 words), excluding the abstract, tables, figures, and in-text and listed references, and section headings. Your paper should pick one country and analyse the country with respect to topics we have discussed together. You may focus on one topic or several.

Complete your work according to the PRESENTATION TEMPLATE posted in the course Blackboard.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission via turn-it-in only in the course blackboard by the due date and time. No other form of submission will be ACCEPTABLE and will be treated as a NON-SUBMISSION. Brisbane time (GMT +10 hours) must be followed.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: The exam will cover the content from all weeks.
